ogTitle: Old Mill vs Old Toongabbie: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Old Mill and Old Toongabbie.
heading: Old Mill vs Old Toongabbie — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Old Mill, New South Wales and Old Toongabbie, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Old Mill has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Old Toongabbie (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Old Mill or Old Toongabbie safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Old Mill has a flood risk score of 26/100, while Old Toongabbie scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Old Mill scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Old Toongabbie scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Old Mill or Old Toongabbie?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Old Mill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
